_note: Local changelog rendered by whats-new.html. No network fetch — privacy-first.

entries.title: Stats, Firefox & a wider dashboard
changes: Added a private, on-device Stats panel: blocked visits, estimated money saved, and calories avoided — with a configurable average meal cost and meal calories. No browsing history is ever stored.
On the block page, pick a suggested recipe with "I'll make this instead" to log the calories you avoided.
The block page now explains exactly why a site was blocked: the domain, rule type, food category, and countries.
FitShield now runs on Firefox from the same single source tree — the shared manifest works in both Chrome and Firefox.
Added a proper FitShield extension icon.
Redesigned Settings to use wide screens better: cards reflow into columns and stack cleanly on narrow windows.
Added Reset options and confirmation dialogs (custom-site removal, resets, and a full factory reset), plus onboarding, local Backup & Restore, and the Chrome Web Store listing.
Added a living, layered background: soft color fields that drift on their own, vary subtly on each open, gain gentle parallax as you scroll, and respect reduced-motion.
Translated the new 0.51 screens (stats, reset, welcome tour, What's New, block reasons, recipe choices) across every supported language, and added German as a new display language.
Cleaned up the repository and consolidated branding assets into a Branding/ folder.

entries.title: Recipe suggestions & blocking fixes
changes: The block page now suggests a vegetarian and a meat/protein recipe based on what you blocked.
Fixed an international domain in the blocklist that was silently disabling all blocking.
Made the block screen reliably appear and show which brand triggered it.

entries.title: Localization
changes: Added 80+ display languages with a searchable language picker and a manual override.
Moved all interface text into the standard Chrome localization system.
